landing.problems_heading: Still figuring it out on your own?
landing.problems_sub: Every parent faces these. Amy Coach gives you a personalized, research-backed step-by-step plan — not generic tips.
landing.not_alone: You're not alone — 87% of AmyNest parents report calmer mornings within 2 weeks.
landing.features_heading: Tools Built on Real Child Science
landing.features_sub: One platform — every feature grounded in AAP guidelines, developmental psychology, and behavioral science.
landing.coach_title: Amy Coach — Solve Real Parenting Challenges
landing.coach_desc: From tantrums to screen time, sleep to focus — Amy Coach gives you deep, personalized 12-step plans grounded in Dr. Ross Greene's CPS model, Dr. Carol Dweck's growth mindset framework, and AAP guidelines. No generic advice. Only what works for your child.
landing.highlight_1: Personalized plans based on your child's profile
landing.feature_routine_desc: Structured, personalized routines that wire your child's brain for consistency — based on habit loop theory.
landing.feature_hub_desc: Expert articles, daily tips, and Amy AI guidance — curated from leading child development research.
landing.feature_ai_desc: Ask anything, get a research-grounded answer instantly. Like having a child psychologist in your pocket.
landing.mid_cta: Try Amy Coach free — your first personalized plan in under 3 minutes.
landing.how_eyebrow: Get Your First Plan in Minutes
landing.how_sub: Three steps to a science-backed plan personalized for your child.
landing.step1_desc: Pick the parenting problem you want to solve — tantrums, sleep, screen time, focus, and more.
landing.step2_desc: Age, personality, and habits — Amy uses this to calibrate a plan to your child's specific profile.
landing.step3_desc: Receive a 12-step, science-backed plan you can start today — built on proven behavioral frameworks.
landing.trust_sub: AmyNest is built on 30+ peer-reviewed frameworks — not opinions. Real science, real results.
landing.trust1_desc: Every routine is designed around Cue → Routine → Reward. Children build lasting habits — not just temporary behavior changes that fade in a week.
landing.trust2_desc: Amy rewards effort over outcome — proven by Stanford research to build resilience, curiosity, and long-term success in children.
landing.trust3_desc: Screen time, sleep schedules, and developmental milestones — all calibrated to American Academy of Pediatrics and CDC guidelines.
landing.final_cta_heading: Your child deserves a plan that actually works.
landing.final_cta_sub: Join 12,000+ families using research-backed parenting plans to raise calmer, smarter, more confident kids — completely free.
landing.whats_new_sub: Four brand-new zones for every age — from infant care to abacus mastery, from coloring fun to printable worksheets.
landing.new_infant_desc: Pediatrician-aligned guidance on sleep, feeding, milestones and daily care for babies under 12 months.
landing.new_abacus_desc: Step-by-step abacus lessons that build mental math and concentration — perfect for ages 5–12.
landing.new_funsheets_desc: Printable activity sheets — mazes, dot-to-dot, tracing and logic puzzles your child can finish today.
landing.new_coloring_desc: Curated coloring books to build focus, fine motor skills and creative expression — print at home or color in-app.

m_2y_names_familiar.label: Names familiar people and objects
m_2y_names_familiar.hint: Family members, favourite toys, body parts.

m_3y_3plus_word_sentence.label: Speaks in 3+ word sentences
m_3y_3plus_word_sentence.hint: Even if grammar is still developing.

m_3y_asks_wh_questions.label: Asks "what / where / why" questions
m_3y_asks_wh_questions.hint: Curiosity is a great sign of language growth.

m_3y_intelligible_family.label: Mostly understandable to family
m_3y_intelligible_family.hint: Close family can understand most of what they say.

m_3y_uses_pronouns.label: Uses pronouns (I, you, me)
m_3y_uses_pronouns.hint: Mixing them up sometimes is completely normal.

m_4plus_full_sentences.label: Speaks in full sentences
m_4plus_full_sentences.hint: Sentences with subject, verb, and detail.

m_4plus_tells_story.label: Tells short stories
m_4plus_tells_story.hint: About their day, a book, or something they imagined.

m_4plus_intelligible_strangers.label: Understandable to strangers
m_4plus_intelligible_strangers.hint: People outside the family can follow what they're saying.

m_4plus_conversation_turns.label: Takes turns in a conversation
m_4plus_conversation_turns.hint: Listens, replies, and stays on the topic.

tongue_exercises.title: Tongue Exercises
tongue_exercises.description: Wiggle the tongue side to side, touch the nose, click the roof of the mouth.

breathing.title: Breathing Exercises
breathing.description: Slow in through the nose, slow out through the mouth — like blowing out candles.

slow_vs_fast.title: Slow vs Fast
slow_vs_fast.description: Say the same sentence really slowly, then a little faster, then super fast.

emotion_express.title: Emotion Expression
emotion_express.description: Say one sentence three ways — happy, sad, surprised.

g_speech_delay_signs.title: Signs of speech delay
g_speech_delay_signs.body: By age 2, most children combine two words. By age 3, family understands most of their speech. If your child is well behind these markers, gentle, early support helps.
g_speech_delay_signs.tip: Trust your instincts — checking in early never hurts and often reassures.

g_screen_time_impact.title: Screen time and language
g_screen_time_impact.body: Passive screen time can slow vocabulary growth in toddlers. Co-watching, narrating, and pausing to ask questions changes that.
g_screen_time_impact.tip: Watch with your child whenever you can — your voice is the secret ingredient.

g_talking_with_toddlers.title: How to talk with toddlers
g_talking_with_toddlers.body: Slow down, use short sentences, look them in the eye, and leave space for them to reply — even if it's a sound, not a word.
g_talking_with_toddlers.tip: Pauses invite words. Count slowly to five before filling the silence.

g_bilingual_development.title: Bilingual speech development
g_bilingual_development.body: Two languages do not cause delay. Children may mix them at first; clarity grows over time. Stay consistent — one parent, one language often works well.
g_bilingual_development.tip: Keep using both languages. Confidence in their first language helps the second.

g_when_to_consult_expert.title: When to consult an expert
g_when_to_consult_expert.body: If you've been concerned for more than a few months, or your child has lost words they used to say, it's worth a chat with a paediatric speech-language professional.
g_when_to_consult_expert.tip: Early support is gentle support — the earlier the conversation, the easier the path.
